(born April 19, 1958), known professionally as **Stevie B**, is an American singer, songwriter, and [record producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") who was influential in the [freestyle](/wiki/Freestyle_music "Freestyle music") and [hi-NRG](/wiki/Hi-NRG "Hi-NRG") dance music scene of the late 1980s, mostly in Miami. He had a 1990 number-one hit [ballad](/wiki/Sentimental_ballad "Sentimental ballad"), "[Because I Love You (The Postman Song)](/wiki/Because_I_Love_You_(The_Postman_Song) "Because I Love You (The Postman Song)")".

American musician and record producer

This article is about the singer. For the bass guitarist, see [Drowning Pool](/wiki/Drowning_Pool "Drowning Pool").

Musical artist

**Steven Bernard Hill** (born April 19, 1958), known professionally as **Stevie B**, is an American singer, songwriter, and [record producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") who was influential in the [freestyle](/wiki/Freestyle_music "Freestyle music") and [hi-NRG](/wiki/Hi-NRG "Hi-NRG") dance music scene of the late 1980s, mostly in Miami. He had a 1990 number-one hit [ballad](/wiki/Sentimental_ballad "Sentimental ballad"), "[Because I Love You (The Postman Song)](/wiki/Because_I_Love_You_(The_Postman_Song) "Because I Love You (The Postman Song)")".

## Early life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stevie_B&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and career")]

Steven Bernard Hill was born on April 19, 1958, in [Fort Lauderdale, Florida](/wiki/Fort_Lauderdale,_Florida "Fort Lauderdale, Florida"). He worked a variety of jobs – from car washing to fast food – before gaining recognition for his 1987 national club smash, "Party Your Body". It was also the title track of his 1988 debut LP, which eventually went gold. In 1989, he had his first top 40 hits with "[I Wanna Be the One](/wiki/I_Wanna_Be_the_One "I Wanna Be the One")", "[In My Eyes](/wiki/In_My_Eyes_(Stevie_B_song) "In My Eyes (Stevie B song)")" and "Love Me For Life". With his 1990 album, *[Love & Emotion](/wiki/Love_%26_Emotion "Love & Emotion")*, he reached the height of his success, with the accompanying single, "Because I Love You" enjoying four weeks at number one on the U.S. [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") in December 1990.[[1]](#cite_note-Ankeny-1) He had two other top 15 hits from *Love and Emotion* with "I'll Be by Your Side" and the title track from the album.[[1]](#cite_note-Ankeny-1)

In 1995, a quasi-greatest hits album was released by Universal Records in the Philippines, simply titled "Dream About You." It contained singles from his previous albums including "Funky Melody", "Running Back", "Waiting for Your Love", among others. It also included as bonus the live version of "Because I Love You" which was recorded in his Tokyo concert, along with a ballad version of "If You Still Love Me." Two more tracks were added as bonus: "You're the One I Think About" and "Quiereme Por Vida." This album likewise featured 2 additional versions of "Dream About You," one with only synthesizers as accompaniment with no drums and percussion, and the other called 'Drop Mix' which prominently featured heavy bass guitar and bass drums.

In 1998, *The Best of Stevie B* was re-released, highlighting the light dance-pop sound that marked the start of his career.

In August 2009, he released *The Terminator* which features the lead single "Running for Miles" and a remixed rendition of one of his biggest hits, "Spring Love", with a guest performance by [Pitbull](/wiki/Pitbull_(rapper) "Pitbull (rapper)") and the music video featuring Polish model Anita Sikorska.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/Stevie_b_performing_in_concert_10-12-19.jpg/250px-Stevie_b_performing_in_concert_10-12-19.jpg)](/wiki/File:Stevie_b_performing_in_concert_10-12-19.jpg)

Stevie b in 2019

On April 10, 2019, Hill took to [Facebook](/wiki/Facebook "Facebook") to make a critical commentary about the lack of freestyle music and the current state of the genre in general.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stevie_B&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

On September 30, 2011, after his concert at [MassMutual Center](/wiki/MassMutual_Center "MassMutual Center") in [Springfield, Massachusetts](/wiki/Springfield,_Massachusetts "Springfield, Massachusetts"), police arrested Hill because he owed $420,000 in unpaid [child support](/wiki/Child_support "Child support").[[4]](#cite_note-Masslive.com-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Comedian [Dulcé Sloan](/wiki/Dulc%C3%A9_Sloan "Dulcé Sloan") is Hill’s niece.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
